  "@context": "https://schema.org",

  "title": "Architect",
  "description": "Jobs Expert, a recruitment services firm, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Architect
Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Interior Design / Architectural

Responsibilities


	Participates in client interview, site visit, follow-on clarification; needs documentation
	Ensures customer needs and priorities are adequately captured during Client Consultation and the proposal development
	Designs solution diagrams (Proficiency in CAD Designs; Proprietary Design Software Designs: Product Designs, Various 2-D &amp; 3-D Designs.
	Detailed Specifications solutions) in accordance with client requirements and/or internal requirements


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: faith.okpara@juwonelectric.com using &quot;Architect&quot; as subject of the email.",

Architect Job at Jobs Expert




        
    

        
        Jobs Expert, a recruitment services firm, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Architect
Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Interior Design / Architectural

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Participates in client interview, site visit, follow-on clarification; needs documentation
	Ensures customer needs and priorities are adequately captured during Client Consultation and the proposal development
	Designs solution diagrams (Proficiency in CAD Designs; Proprietary Design Software Designs: Product Designs, Various 2-D & 3-D Designs.
	Detailed Specifications solutions) in accordance with client requirements and/or internal requirements


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Architect" as subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Lead",
  "description": "Owens and Xley - Our client, a growing brand in the retail fashion space, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Lead

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	Our client is seeking an experienced and results-driven Sales Lead to drive revenue growth and expand market presence across physical and digital channels.
	The ideal candidate will combine strong commercial acumen with leadership capabilities to develop strategic sales initiatives, build valuable partnerships, and strengthen customer engagement while maintaining an exceptional brand experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead and coordinate sales activities to achieve revenue and performance targets.
	Develop and execute sales strategies aligned with business growth objectives.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities, partnerships, and collaborations.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, wholesale partners, and key stakeholders.
	Manage wholesale accounts and support the expansion of third-party stockists.
	Analyze market trends, customer insights, and competitor activities to guide sales strategies.
	Collaborate with marketing teams to align campaigns and product launches with sales objectives.
	Drive growth across retail and digital sales channels.
	Ensure high customer service standards across all touchpoints.
	Monitor sales performance and provide insights and recommendations for improvement.
	Support the training, development, and motivation of the sales team.


Requirements


	Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Commerce, or a related field.
	Minimum of 7 years of progressive sales experience, including at least 2&ndash;3 years in a leadership role.
	Proven track record of achieving and exceeding sales targets.
	Experience managing both B2B and B2C sales channels.
	Strong understanding of market trends, consumer behavior, and sales analytics.
	Experience in business development, wholesale management, and partnership building.
	Excellent leadership, communication, negotiation, and relationship-management skills.
	Proficiency with CRM tools, sales software, and digital commerce platforms.
	Strong analytical, strategic thinking, and problem-solving abilities.


Salary
N800,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should Send their applications to: recruitmentng@harleyreed.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-16",

Sales Lead Job at Owens and Xley




        
    

        
        Owens and Xley - Our client, a growing brand in the retail fashion space, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Lead

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our client is seeking an experienced and results-driven Sales Lead to drive revenue growth and expand market presence across physical and digital channels.
	The ideal candidate will combine strong commercial acumen with leadership capabilities to develop strategic sales initiatives, build valuable partnerships, and strengthen customer engagement while maintaining an exceptional brand experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead and coordinate sales activities to achieve revenue and performance targets.
	Develop and execute sales strategies aligned with business growth objectives.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities, partnerships, and collaborations.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, wholesale partners, and key stakeholders.
	Manage wholesale accounts and support the expansion of third-party stockists.
	Analyze market trends, customer insights, and competitor activities to guide sales strategies.
	Collaborate with marketing teams to align campaigns and product launches with sales objectives.
	Drive growth across retail and digital sales channels.
	Ensure high customer service standards across all touchpoints.
	Monitor sales performance and provide insights and recommendations for improvement.
	Support the training, development, and motivation of the sales team.


Requirements


	Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Commerce, or a related field.
	Minimum of 7 years of progressive sales experience, including at least 2–3 years in a leadership role.
	Proven track record of achieving and exceeding sales targets.
	Experience managing both B2B and B2C sales channels.
	Strong understanding of market trends, consumer behavior, and sales analytics.
	Experience in business development, wholesale management, and partnership building.
	Excellent leadership, communication, negotiation, and relationship-management skills.
	Proficiency with CRM tools, sales software, and digital commerce platforms.
	Strong analytical, strategic thinking, and problem-solving abilities.


Salary
N800,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should Send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HSE Officer",
  "description": "Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited (formerly Moody International Nigeria Limited) was established in 1996 as a partnership between Moody International Group and Nigerian investors. Since commencing operations in March 1997 the company continues to grow and exerts its presence in numerous Projects in the Nigeria Oil and Gas Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Description


	Assist in the follow-up of site personnel (company and contractor) HSE training and preparedness, ensuring adequate attention is drawn to any lack of compliance.
	Conduct regular check on the state of all HSE equipment and material to ensure sound functioning and readiness for deployment in case of emergency. Taking part in HSE studies and risk assessment (JRA) for the execution of operations
	Assist the Head of HSE in the follow-up of HSE MS and procedures and regular updating of HSE records / performance indicators and providing such as at when required for reporting purposes. Checking enforcement, efficiency and maintenance of HSE MS. Ensuring that all HSE activities are fully documented and reported as necessary.
	Participates actively in all HSE Committee meeting, site planning, and coordination meetings as well as contributing to defining ways and means to identify / minimizing risks and the impact of activities, especially SIMOPS.
	Ensures that stocks of necessary HSE consumables, e.g., hand gloves, earmuffs/plugs, disposable coverall, spare fire extinguishers, are maintained at appropriate levels.
	Supervises the reporting of incidents/accidents and anomalies and follows up on the close out of actions from investigation recommendations.
	Regularly input /update Events in Synergi.
	Organizes, motivates and develops operations and maintenance staff to report anomalies. Contributes to the completion of PTW and the documents needed prior to operations and see that prescriptions are applied. Ensuring HSE performance monitoring.
	Carries out any other duties as may be assigned by the Head of HSE


Accountabilities


	Working within a team of HSE and Operation personnel, oversees compliance to HSE-established guidelines and procedures and referring exceptions to the Head of HSE but makes specific decisions relating to organization of HSE related activities


Job Requirementsd


	Minimum of an OND / HND qualification in Engineering or Physical Sciences.
	5 - 10 years of experience in oilfield production operations.
	Sound grasp and understanding of safe working practices required in the oil industry.
	Good Supervisory Skills.

HSE Officer Job at Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Amaiden Energy Nigeria Limited (formerly Moody International Nigeria Limited) was established in 1996 as a partnership between Moody International Group and Nigerian investors. Since commencing operations in March 1997 the company continues to grow and exerts its presence in numerous Projects in the Nigeria Oil and Gas Industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in the follow-up of site personnel (company and contractor) HSE training and preparedness, ensuring adequate attention is drawn to any lack of compliance.
	Conduct regular check on the state of all HSE equipment and material to ensure sound functioning and readiness for deployment in case of emergency. Taking part in HSE studies and risk assessment (JRA) for the execution of operations
	Assist the Head of HSE in the follow-up of HSE MS and procedures and regular updating of HSE records / performance indicators and providing such as at when required for reporting purposes. Checking enforcement, efficiency and maintenance of HSE MS. Ensuring that all HSE activities are fully documented and reported as necessary.
	Participates actively in all HSE Committee meeting, site planning, and coordination meetings as well as contributing to defining ways and means to identify / minimizing risks and the impact of activities, especially SIMOPS.
	Ensures that stocks of necessary HSE consumables, e.g., hand gloves, earmuffs/plugs, disposable coverall, spare fire extinguishers, are maintained at appropriate levels.
	Supervises the reporting of incidents/accidents and anomalies and follows up on the close out of actions from investigation recommendations.
	Regularly input /update Events in Synergi.
	Organizes, motivates and develops operations and maintenance staff to report anomalies. Contributes to the completion of PTW and the documents needed prior to operations and see that prescriptions are applied. Ensuring HSE performance monitoring.
	Carries out any other duties as may be assigned by the Head of HSE


Accountabilities


	Working within a team of HSE and Operation personnel, oversees compliance to HSE-established guidelines and procedures and referring exceptions to the Head of HSE but makes specific decisions relating to organization of HSE related activities


Job Requirementsd


	Minimum of an OND / HND qualification in Engineering or Physical Sciences.
	5 - 10 years of experience in oilfield production operations.
	Sound grasp and understanding of safe working practices required in the oil industry.
	Good Supervisory Skills.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Lead",
  "description": "Owens and Xley - Our client, a growing brand in the retail fashion space, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Lead

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	Our client is seeking an experienced and results-driven Sales Lead to drive revenue growth and expand market presence across physical and digital channels.
	The ideal candidate will combine strong commercial acumen with leadership capabilities to develop strategic sales initiatives, build valuable partnerships, and strengthen customer engagement while maintaining an exceptional brand experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead and coordinate sales activities to achieve revenue and performance targets.
	Develop and execute sales strategies aligned with business growth objectives.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities, partnerships, and collaborations.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, wholesale partners, and key stakeholders.
	Manage wholesale accounts and support the expansion of third-party stockists.
	Analyze market trends, customer insights, and competitor activities to guide sales strategies.
	Collaborate with marketing teams to align campaigns and product launches with sales objectives.
	Drive growth across retail and digital sales channels.
	Ensure high customer service standards across all touchpoints.
	Monitor sales performance and provide insights and recommendations for improvement.
	Support the training, development, and motivation of the sales team.


Requirements


	Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Commerce, or a related field.
	Minimum of 7 years of progressive sales experience, including at least 2&ndash;3 years in a leadership role.
	Proven track record of achieving and exceeding sales targets.
	Experience managing both B2B and B2C sales channels.
	Strong understanding of market trends, consumer behavior, and sales analytics.
	Experience in business development, wholesale management, and partnership building.
	Excellent leadership, communication, negotiation, and relationship-management skills.
	Proficiency with CRM tools, sales software, and digital commerce platforms.
	Strong analytical, strategic thinking, and problem-solving abilities.


Salary
N800,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should Send their applications to: recruitmentng@harleyreed.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Sales Lead Job at Owens and Xley




        
    

        
        Owens and Xley - Our client, a growing brand in the retail fashion space, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Lead

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our client is seeking an experienced and results-driven Sales Lead to drive revenue growth and expand market presence across physical and digital channels.
	The ideal candidate will combine strong commercial acumen with leadership capabilities to develop strategic sales initiatives, build valuable partnerships, and strengthen customer engagement while maintaining an exceptional brand experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead and coordinate sales activities to achieve revenue and performance targets.
	Develop and execute sales strategies aligned with business growth objectives.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities, partnerships, and collaborations.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, wholesale partners, and key stakeholders.
	Manage wholesale accounts and support the expansion of third-party stockists.
	Analyze market trends, customer insights, and competitor activities to guide sales strategies.
	Collaborate with marketing teams to align campaigns and product launches with sales objectives.
	Drive growth across retail and digital sales channels.
	Ensure high customer service standards across all touchpoints.
	Monitor sales performance and provide insights and recommendations for improvement.
	Support the training, development, and motivation of the sales team.


Requirements


	Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Commerce, or a related field.
	Minimum of 7 years of progressive sales experience, including at least 2–3 years in a leadership role.
	Proven track record of achieving and exceeding sales targets.
	Experience managing both B2B and B2C sales channels.
	Strong understanding of market trends, consumer behavior, and sales analytics.
	Experience in business development, wholesale management, and partnership building.
	Excellent leadership, communication, negotiation, and relationship-management skills.
	Proficiency with CRM tools, sales software, and digital commerce platforms.
	Strong analytical, strategic thinking, and problem-solving abilities.


Salary
N800,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should Send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Manager",
  "description": "Magabby Logistics is a fast-growing logistics company committed to providing efficient, reliable, and professional shipping and delivery solutions for individuals and businesses. With a strong focus on customer satisfaction and operational excellence, the company continues to expand its services and presence within and beyond Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Manager
Job Location: Ketu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Oversee the daily operations of the customer service team.
	Lead, train, and supervise team members to deliver outstanding service.
	Develop and implement customer service strategies, standards, and processes.
	Monitor team performance and ensure achievement of KPIs and targets.
	Handle escalated customer issues professionally and ensure timely resolution.
	Build strong customer relationships and drive customer retention.
	Provide regular reports on customer service activities, performance, and improvements.
	Collaborate with other departments to ensure smooth service delivery.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a B.Sc Degree proven experience as a Customr Service Manager or in a similar leadership role.
	Strong background in handling and managing customer service teams.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and conflict-resolution skills.
	Strong leadership and decision-making abilities.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.
	Proficient in customer service tools and reporting systems.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: Hr@myafrimall.com using &ldquo;Customer Service Manager &ndash; Magabby&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Manager Job at Magabby Logistics




        
    

        
        Magabby Logistics is a fast-growing logistics company committed to providing efficient, reliable, and professional shipping and delivery solutions for individuals and businesses. With a strong focus on customer satisfaction and operational excellence, the company continues to expand its services and presence within and beyond Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Manager
Job Location: Ketu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee the daily operations of the customer service team.
	Lead, train, and supervise team members to deliver outstanding service.
	Develop and implement customer service strategies, standards, and processes.
	Monitor team performance and ensure achievement of KPIs and targets.
	Handle escalated customer issues professionally and ensure timely resolution.
	Build strong customer relationships and drive customer retention.
	Provide regular reports on customer service activities, performance, and improvements.
	Collaborate with other departments to ensure smooth service delivery.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a B.Sc Degree proven experience as a Customr Service Manager or in a similar leadership role.
	Strong background in handling and managing customer service teams.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and conflict-resolution skills.
	Strong leadership and decision-making abilities.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.
	Proficient in customer service tools and reporting systems.


Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Customer Service Manager – Magabby” as the subject of the mail.
